objective: Measure whether VPI Remap can move from static diagnostic maps to dynamic per-frame maps that could support future mesh/local-warp EIS work, while keeping the current Orin NX MMAPI/VPI/NVENC dataflow and quality boundaries honest.

entry_context.project_positioning: Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization, with EIS as the representative workload.
entry_context.latest_positive_result: Remap pad/crop can use VPI_IMAGE_BUFFER_NVBUFFER wrappers on format-matched pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch and shows a small diagnostic stage-average gain.
entry_context.latest_negative_result: Remap pad/crop stream-only reuse is healthy but slower, so it must not be carried forward as an optimization candidate.
entry_context.current_question: If Remap maps must change per frame, what is the cost and API boundary for rebuilding map data and/or Remap payloads, and can a device-stage diagnostic still run under native-size pad/crop semantics?
entry_context.quality_boundary: Do not change resid_r15_s07, do not claim Remap improves EIS quality, and do not reopen global affine/rigid tuning.

frozen_variables: Dynamic Remap is measured first as operator/dataflow capability
Remap stream-only reuse remains negative evidence, not an active candidate
MMAPI Remap diagnostics keep 640x360 main chain and 640x368 VPI scratch unless a measured API constraint requires otherwise
No mesh/local-warp quality claim without measured quality verifier

result.decision: Dynamic Remap is viable in standalone and MMAPI pad/crop diagnostics, but per-frame payload rebuild is a material cost.
result.api_boundary: Local VPI C API exposes vpiCreateRemap and vpiSubmitRemap; no in-place Remap payload update API was found.

matrix_direction.decision: VPIWarpMap stores output-to-input coordinates; source_to_dest matrices must be inverted before filling keypoints.

result.claim_boundary: Operator/dataflow cost boundary only. No EIS quality improvement, no mesh/local-warp solved, no zero-copy, and no full real-time EIS.

task_queue.title: Contract and negative-route cleanup
task_queue.objective: Register this loop and mark Remap stream-only reuse as negative evidence rather than a future optimization candidate.
success_criteria: onboard points to this contract while active
doctor passes
Remap stream-only is documented as healthy but slower
Running main-gate claim remains forbidden

task_queue.title: VPI Remap API boundary
task_queue.objective: Inspect local VPI headers/samples/docs enough to determine whether map data or payload can be reused or must be rebuilt per frame.
success_criteria: vpiWarpMap data ownership is understood
payload creation and reuse boundary is stated
dynamic update route is selected before coding

task_queue.title: Standalone dynamic Remap probe
task_queue.objective: Build a C++ probe that measures static payload reuse versus per-frame WarpMap/payload rebuild cost on BGR8 and NV12_ER.
success_criteria: probe compiles on Jetson
identity/static mode passes
dynamic recreate mode passes or failure is classified
CSV records map build, payload create, submit/sync, and total timing

task_queue.title: Matrix-to-WarpMap correctness
task_queue.objective: Convert representative 3x3 EIS matrices into Remap keypoints and verify map direction against an OpenCV remap or image-level sanity check.
success_criteria: source-to-dest versus output-to-input convention is explicit
identity matrix produces identity output
a non-identity matrix produces an explainable direction
black-border behavior is classified

task_queue.title: MMAPI dynamic Remap diagnostic
task_queue.objective: If P2/P3 are viable, run a minimal MMAPI Remap diagnostic with dynamic per-frame payload/map behavior under native-size pad/crop semantics.
success_criteria: short run returns rc=0
full 180-frame run returns rc=0 if short run succeeds
stage timing separates map/payload cost when possible
output readability and black-border are checked
stop_reasons: standalone dynamic Remap cost is already prohibitive
MMAPI route requires broad rewrite before a minimal verifier

task_queue.title: NvBuffer dynamic Remap branch
task_queue.objective: Only if P4 is viable, test whether the existing Remap NvBuffer wrapper route remains viable for dynamic maps.
success_criteria: NvBuffer branch is attempted only after EGLImage dynamic path is classified
rc/readability/timing are recorded
small gains or failure boundary are stated

task_queue.title: Knowledge recovery if blocked
task_queue.objective: Use local knowledge, public/official docs, and an internal-AI prompt if the dynamic Remap API route remains unclear.
success_criteria: blocker is plain-language
local attempts are listed
internal-AI prompt is produced only if needed

task_queue.title: Evidence closeout
task_queue.objective: Write measured dynamic Remap result and claim boundary into concise docs.
success_criteria: report contains only measured data

review assets are copied to the categorized Windows review tree

task_queue.title: Final validation and commit
task_queue.objective: Run JSON, harness, diff, secret, and artifact checks, then commit coherent source/config/docs changes.
success_criteria: JSON parses
doctor passes
git diff --check passes
large artifacts and secrets remain untracked
oral template remains unstaged
commit message trailer policy is followed

forbidden_claims: full-pipeline acceleration from dynamic Remap operator timing alone
stop_reasons: Jetson SSH or device access is unavailable and cannot be recovered locally
VPI Remap API requires per-frame rebuild cost that is too high for current project scope and no lower-cost route is found
matrix-to-WarpMap convention cannot be validated after focused local and reference search
